metadata.title: Claude Code Usage Guide | AI Coding Agent for Terminal, IDE & Web
metadata.description: Master Claude Code with comprehensive guides, practical tutorials, and real-world examples. Available in Terminal, VS Code, JetBrains, Desktop App, and the Web.
metadata.keywords: Claude Code, AI coding, terminal, VS Code, JetBrains, Desktop App, development tools, programming, artificial intelligence, coding agent, MCP, subagents, skills, hooks

home.title: Claude Code is Available Everywhere
home.subtitle: Terminal, VS Code, JetBrains, Desktop App, and Web. Build features, fix bugs, create PRs, connect tools via MCP, customize with skills and hooks, run agent teams, and automate everything.

features.title: What You Can Do with Claude Code
features.subtitle: A full-featured AI coding agent that works wherever you do

aiPowered.title: Build & Ship Features
aiPowered.description: Build entire features, fix bugs, refactor code, create pull requests, and generate documentation — all from natural language instructions.

terminalIntegration.title: Works Everywhere
terminalIntegration.description: Terminal CLI, VS Code extension, JetBrains plugin, standalone Desktop App, and the Web at claude.ai/code. Use it however you prefer.

multilingual.title: Extensible & Customizable
multilingual.description: Connect tools via MCP, create custom skills and hooks, build plugin packages, run parallel agent teams, and pipe/script/automate workflows.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.subtitle: Get Claude Code running in seconds

useCases.title: Key Capabilities
useCases.subtitle: From everyday coding tasks to complex multi-agent workflows

codeReview.title: Code Review & Refactoring
codeReview.description: Analyze code quality, suggest improvements, perform large-scale refactors. Use subagents for parallel review across multiple files.

testGeneration.title: Test Generation & Bug Fixing
testGeneration.description: Generate unit tests, integration tests, and E2E tests. Debug issues with extended thinking and automatic verification.

documentation.title: PRs, Docs & Git Automation
documentation.description: Create pull requests with summaries, generate documentation, write commit messages, and automate your entire Git workflow.

debugging.title: MCP Tools & Integrations
debugging.description: Connect to databases, GitHub, Slack, browsers, and 100+ tools via Model Context Protocol. Extend Claude's reach beyond the filesystem.

platforms.title: Available Platforms
platforms.terminalDesc: The original command-line experience with full power
platforms.vscodeDesc: Native sidebar integration in Visual Studio Code
platforms.jetbrainsDesc: IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, and all JetBrains IDEs
platforms.desktopDesc: Standalone application for macOS and Windows
platforms.webDesc: No local setup needed — runs in your browser

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started with Claude Code
gettingStarted.subtitle: Choose your platform and get coding with Claude in minutes.

systemRequirementsDetail.windowsWarning: Claude Code runs natively on Windows. Git for Windows is required. You can also use WSL.

installationDetail.nodeRequirement: Git must be installed. On Windows, install Git for Windows. Node.js is no longer required for installation.
installationDetail.subscriptionInfo: Claude Pro, Max, Teams, Enterprise, or API Console account required
installationDetail.npmDeprecatedInfo: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code is no longer recommended. Use the native install methods above.
installationDetail.vscodeExtension: VS Code Extension: Install from the VS Code Marketplace (search 'Claude Code')
installationDetail.jetbrainsPlugin: JetBrains Plugin: Install from the JetBrains Marketplace
installationDetail.desktopApp: Desktop App: Download from claude.ai/download for macOS and Windows
installationDetail.webApp: Web App: Visit claude.ai/code — no installation needed

authenticationDetail.oauthLogin: Run 'claude' and follow the browser-based OAuth login
authenticationDetail.apiKey: Or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable for API access
authenticationDetail.enterpriseSetup: Enterprise users: your admin configures SSO and managed policies

firstProjectDetail.readyToStart: You're ready to start building with Claude Code.
firstProjectDetail.exploreCodebase: Ask Claude to explain the project structure
firstProjectDetail.makeChange: Ask Claude to make a small change and review the diff
firstProjectDetail.runTests: Ask Claude to run tests and fix any failures

examples.askCode: Explore code: 'explain how authentication works in this project'
examples.editFiles: Edit files: 'add input validation to the signup form'
examples.runTests: Run tests: 'run tests and fix any failures'
examples.extendedThinking: Deep thinking: 'think deeply about this architecture' or 'ultrathink'
examples.gitAutomation: Git automation: 'create a PR for these changes'
examples.webSearch: Use tools: Claude auto-searches docs, browses the web, and uses MCP servers

usageGuide.title: Complete Claude Code CLI Reference
usageGuide.subtitle: Every command, flag, keyboard shortcut, and interactive feature in Claude Code.

basicCommandsDetail.description: Core commands to interact with Claude Code from the terminal.

fileAnalysis.title: Print Mode (Non-Interactive)
fileAnalysis.description: claude -p "query" — Output response and exit. Perfect for scripts and piping.

printMode.title: Print Mode (Non-Interactive)
printMode.description: claude -p "query" — Output response and exit. Perfect for scripts and piping.

continueSession.title: Continue Session
continueSession.description: claude -c — Continue your most recent conversation

resumeSession.title: Resume Session
resumeSession.description: claude -r — Pick from recent conversations to resume

updateClaude.title: Update
updateClaude.description: claude update — Update Claude Code to the latest version

mcpCommand.title: MCP
mcpCommand.description: claude mcp add/remove/list — Manage MCP servers

remoteControl.title: Remote Control
remoteControl.description: claude remote-control — Enable remote control for external integrations

coreWorkflowsDetail.description: Keyboard shortcuts and interactive features available during a Claude session.

projectExploration.title: Plan Mode (Shift+Tab)
projectExploration.description: Toggle Plan Mode to make Claude think and plan before writing code. Claude explores, analyzes, and proposes a plan for your approval.

smartEditing.title: Voice Dictation & Bash Mode
smartEditing.description: Hold Space for voice dictation. Type ! for inline shell commands. Use @ for file references.

gitWorkflow.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
gitWorkflow.description: Ctrl+C: Cancel. Ctrl+G: Fuzzy search. Ctrl+B: Background agent. Ctrl+T: Task list. Shift+Tab: Plan Mode.

keyboardShortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
keyboardShortcuts.description: Ctrl+C: Cancel current generation. Ctrl+G: Fuzzy search for files. Ctrl+O: Open output in editor. Ctrl+R: Redo last prompt. Ctrl+B: Run subagent in background. Ctrl+T: Open task list. Shift+Tab: Toggle Plan Mode. Esc: Cancel input or interrupt.

planMode.title: Plan Mode (Shift+Tab)
planMode.description: Toggle Plan Mode to make Claude think and plan before writing code. Claude explores, analyzes, and proposes a plan for your approval before making changes.

vimMode.title: Vim Mode
vimMode.description: Enable Vim-style keybindings for the input area via /vim command or settings.

multilineInput.title: Multiline Input
multilineInput.description: Use \\ at end of line to continue, or paste multiline text directly. Shift+Enter also works in some terminals.

voiceDictation.title: Voice Dictation
voiceDictation.description: Hold Space to dictate voice input. Claude handles transcription typos gracefully.

btw.title: Side Questions with /btw
btw.description: Use /btw to ask a side question without losing the main conversation context.

bashMode.title: Bash Mode (! prefix)
bashMode.description: Type ! followed by a shell command to run it directly without leaving Claude. Example: !git status

fileReferences.title: File References with @
fileReferences.description: Use @ to reference files directly in your prompt. Claude reads the file content automatically.

promptSuggestions.title: Prompt Suggestions
promptSuggestions.description: Claude offers contextual suggestions based on your project and recent actions.

advancedFeaturesDetail.description: Power features for advanced workflows and automation.

deepThinking.title: Extended Thinking
deepThinking.description: Use 'think', 'think hard', 'think harder', or 'ultrathink' to activate deeper reasoning. More thinking budget = more thorough analysis for complex problems.

projectMemory.title: Project Memory (CLAUDE.md)
projectMemory.description: Create CLAUDE.md files to store project rules, conventions, and context. Claude reads these automatically. Keep them short, specific, and regularly pruned.

realtimeSearch.title: Checkpoints & Rewind
realtimeSearch.description: Claude automatically creates checkpoints. Use /rewind to go back to any previous state if something goes wrong.

checkpoints.title: Checkpoints & Rewind
checkpoints.description: Claude automatically creates checkpoints. Use /rewind to go back to any previous state if something goes wrong.

imageAnalysis.title: Image & Screenshot Analysis
imageAnalysis.description: Paste images or screenshots directly into Claude. Drag and drop, or use @ to reference image files. Claude can analyze UIs and generate code from designs.

worktrees.title: Git Worktrees
worktrees.description: Use --worktree flag to run Claude in a separate git worktree for isolated parallel sessions. Each session gets its own working directory.

backgroundCommands.title: Background Bash Commands
backgroundCommands.description: Long-running shell commands run in the background. Claude continues working while commands execute and checks results when ready.

prReview.title: PR Review Status
prReview.description: When working on a PR, Claude shows review status in the footer. See pending reviews, approvals, and requested changes at a glance.

cliOptionsDetail.description: Comprehensive list of CLI flags and options for customizing Claude Code behavior.

builtinCommands.title: Model & Execution Flags
builtinCommands.description: --model (-m): Select model. --effort: Set thinking effort. --max-turns: Limit turns. --max-budget-usd: Set spending cap. --agent: Use a subagent.

executionOptions.title: System Prompt & Output Flags
executionOptions.description: --system-prompt: Override prompt. --output-format (text/json/stream-json): Control output. --json-schema: Validate JSON.

environmentSetup.title: Tool & Session Flags
environmentSetup.description: --allowedTools/--disallowedTools: Control tools. --worktree: Git isolation. --chrome: Chrome integration. --plugin-dir: Load plugins.

modelFlags.title: Model & Execution Flags
modelFlags.description: --model (-m): Select model. --effort: Set thinking effort (low/medium/high). --max-turns: Limit conversation turns. --max-budget-usd: Set spending cap. --agent: Use a specific subagent for entire session.

directoryFlags.title: Directory & Scope Flags
directoryFlags.description: --add-dir: Add extra directories to context. --worktree: Run in git worktree. --remote: Run remotely. --remote-control: Enable remote control.

systemPromptFlags.title: System Prompt Flags
systemPromptFlags.description: --system-prompt: Override system prompt. --system-prompt-file: Load from file. --append-system-prompt: Append to default. --append-system-prompt-file: Append from file.

outputFlags.title: Output & Format Flags
outputFlags.description: --output-format (text/json/stream-json): Control output format. --json-schema: Validate JSON output. --verbose: Show detailed logging.

toolFlags.title: Tool & Permission Flags
toolFlags.description: --allowedTools: Restrict available tools. --disallowedTools: Block specific tools. --tools: Specify tool configuration. --permission-mode: Set permission behavior. --teleport: Enable IDE teleporting.

sessionFlags.title: Session Flags
sessionFlags.description: --chrome: Launch with Chrome integration. --teammate-mode: Enable teammate mode. --agents: Load additional agent definitions. --plugin-dir: Load plugins from directory.

slashCommands.title: Slash Commands
slashCommands.description: Built-in commands available during an interactive session.

commands.overview: give me an overview of this codebase
commands.authFiles: find the files that handle user authentication
commands.npmTestError: run the tests and fix any failures
commands.fixTsIgnore: fix all the @ts-ignore comments with proper types
commands.findDeprecated: find deprecated API usage and suggest replacements
commands.refactorUtils: refactor utils.js to use modern ES2024 features

specificInstructions.title: Be Specific
specificInstructions.description: Instead of 'fix the error', say 'fix the null reference error in the handleSubmit function in user.tsx'.

incrementalDevelopment.title: Use Plan Mode First
incrementalDevelopment.description: Press Shift+Tab to enable Plan Mode. Let Claude explore and plan before making changes.

projectMemoryTip.title: Manage Context
projectMemoryTip.description: Use /clear to start fresh, /compact to compress, and /btw for side questions. Keep CLAUDE.md short and specific.

skillHotReload.title: Agent Teams
skillHotReload.description: Run parallel agent teams with shared task lists and mailboxes

asyncAgents.title: Background Subagents
asyncAgents.description: Run agents in background with Ctrl+B, check results later

mcpSearch.title: MCP Tool Search
mcpSearch.description: Auto-deferred MCP tools save context window space

language.title: Desktop & Web
language.description: Desktop App for macOS/Windows and Web at claude.ai/code

planCommand.title: Worktree Isolation
planCommand.description: Run agents in isolated git worktrees with --worktree flag

tutorials.title: Claude Code Practical Tutorials
tutorials.subtitle: Step-by-step guides for real-world development workflows with Claude Code.

resumeConversations.title: Resume Conversations
resumeConversations.description: Continue where you left off or resume any previous session.
resumeConversations.useSelector: claude -r to pick from recent sessions
resumeConversations.tip: Use /memory to review session context before resuming. Use /compact to compress long conversations.

understandingCodebases.title: Exploring Codebases
understandingCodebases.description: Quickly understand new projects or complex codebases with Claude.
understandingCodebases.getOverview: Ask for an overview of the project
understandingCodebases.findFeatures: Use Ctrl+G to fuzzy-search for files
understandingCodebases.tip: Start broad ('explain this project') then narrow down ('how does authentication work'). Use @ to reference specific files.

bugFixing.title: Bug Fixing Workflow
bugFixing.description: Describe the bug. Claude explores the codebase, identifies the root cause, proposes a fix, writes tests to verify, and creates a PR.

codeRefactoring.title: Code Refactoring
codeRefactoring.description: Use Plan Mode (Shift+Tab) first to explore and plan. Claude analyzes the code, proposes a refactoring strategy, and executes it incrementally.

testing.title: Test Writing
testing.description: Ask Claude to write tests for your code. It analyzes the implementation, generates comprehensive test cases, and runs them to verify they pass.

prCreation.title: Pull Request Creation
prCreation.description: After making changes, ask Claude to create a PR. It writes a summary, adds test plans, and opens the PR via GitHub CLI.

documentation.title: Documentation Generation
documentation.description: Generate README files, API docs, and inline comments. Claude reads your code and produces accurate, up-to-date documentation.

imageWork.title: Image & Screenshot Analysis
imageWork.description: Paste screenshots of UIs, error messages, or design mockups. Claude analyzes them and generates code or debugging steps.
imageWork.dragDrop: Drag and drop images directly into the terminal, or use @ to reference image files

planMode.title: Plan Mode Workflow
planMode.description: Press Shift+Tab to toggle Plan Mode. Claude explores the codebase, analyzes the problem, and proposes a plan for your approval before writing any code.

extendedThinking.title: Extended Thinking
extendedThinking.description: Use keywords like 'think', 'think hard', or 'ultrathink' to give Claude more thinking budget for complex architectural decisions and deep analysis.

worktrees.title: Git Worktrees for Parallel Sessions
worktrees.description: Use --worktree flag to run multiple Claude sessions on the same repo without conflicts. Each session gets an isolated working directory.

nonInteractive.title: Non-Interactive Mode
nonInteractive.description: Use claude -p for scripting and automation. Pipe input, chain commands, and integrate Claude into CI/CD pipelines.

fanOut.title: Fan-Out Patterns
fanOut.description: Use subagents to process multiple files or tasks in parallel. The main agent coordinates while subagents handle individual pieces.

startNow.title: Start Now
startNow.description: Get hands-on with Claude Code's powerful features.

tips.title: Real-World Tips
tips.subtitle: Battle-tested techniques for getting the most out of Claude Code
tips.mainSubtitle: Proven techniques from power users and the official best practices guide
tips.detailDescription: These tips combine community-tested techniques with official Anthropic guidance for maximum productivity.
tips.sourceCredit: Based on community tips and official documentation

tip1.title: Give Claude Verification Criteria
tip1.description: The single most impactful thing you can do: tell Claude how to verify its own work. Tests, type checks, screenshots, linting — give Claude a way to confirm success.

practices.freshConversation: Include test commands: 'fix the bug and make sure all tests pass'
practices.splitTasks: Provide verification steps: 'run the linter after making changes'
practices.avoidOverload: Use screenshots for visual verification with /chrome or Playwright MCP

tip1.quote: The most effective prompts include success criteria, not just instructions.

tip2.title: Explore First, Then Plan, Then Code
tip2.description: Don't jump straight into code changes. Let Claude understand the codebase first, then plan the approach, then execute.

practices.breakDown: Start with 'explore this codebase and explain the architecture'
practices.furtherBreak: Use Plan Mode (Shift+Tab) for complex tasks
practices.oneAtATime: Review the plan before letting Claude write code

tip2.quote: The explore-plan-code workflow prevents most failures before they happen.

tip3.title: Provide Specific Context with @ and Images
tip3.description: Reference specific files with @filename, paste screenshots and error images, and give Claude exactly the context it needs.

practices.understand: Use @ to reference specific files in your prompts
practices.research: Paste screenshots of errors, UIs, or designs
practices.architecture: Copy-paste error messages and stack traces directly
practices.prepare: Reference documentation URLs for Claude to fetch

tip3.quote: The more specific your context, the more accurate Claude's response.

tip4.title: Write Effective CLAUDE.md Files
tip4.description: Your CLAUDE.md is the single most important configuration. Keep it short, specific, and regularly pruned. Bad CLAUDE.md = bad results.

practices.autoCommit: Keep it concise — under 500 lines for project-level
practices.allowPull: Include build/test/lint commands Claude should use
practices.gitBisect: Add code style preferences and conventions
practices.ghCli: Prune regularly — remove outdated or redundant instructions

tip4.warning: A long, unfocused CLAUDE.md hurts more than it helps. Quality over quantity.

tip5.title: Manage Context Aggressively
tip5.description: Context window management is critical. Use /clear, /compact, /btw, and fresh sessions to keep Claude performing at its best.

practices.writeTests: Start fresh conversations for new topics (/clear)
practices.draftPR: Use /btw for side questions that don't pollute main context
practices.manualReview: Use subagents for isolated investigation tasks

tip5.quote: AI context is like milk — best served fresh and condensed.

tip6.title: Use Subagents for Investigation
tip6.description: Spawn subagents to explore, investigate, and research without consuming your main session's context. Results come back summarized.

practices.doubleCheck: Use the Explore subagent (Haiku, read-only) for quick lookups
practices.verifyTable: Use the Plan subagent for complex analysis without code changes
practices.playwright: Create custom subagents for recurring investigation patterns
practices.tmux: Run subagents in background with Ctrl+B

tip6.claudeMdTip: Subagents have their own context windows, so they don't consume tokens from your main session.

tip7.title: Rewind with Checkpoints
tip7.description: Claude automatically creates git checkpoints. Use /rewind to go back if something goes wrong. Course-correct early with Esc.

practices.showBranch: Press Esc early if Claude is going in the wrong direction
practices.tokenBar: Check git diff after changes to verify correctness
practices.syncStatus: Use /clear to reset if the conversation derails

tip7.quote: Course-correct early. The cost of rewinding goes up with every additional change.

tip8.title: Configure Permissions and Hooks
tip8.description: Set up permission modes and hooks to automate repetitive approvals and enforce team standards.

practices.handoffDoc: Use permission modes: default, acceptEdits, dontAsk, plan
practices.includeTrials: Set up hooks for auto-formatting after edits
practices.freshStart: Use hooks to enforce security policies on tool usage

tip8.handoffPrompt: Hooks turn repetitive manual approvals into automated guardrails.

tip9.title: Use CLI Tools and MCP Servers
tip9.description: Claude works best when it has access to the right tools. Connect GitHub CLI, AWS CLI, database tools, and MCP servers.

practices.localTranscription: Install gh (GitHub CLI) for PR and issue management
practices.fasterComm: Connect MCP servers for databases, Slack, and more
practices.likePhoneCall: Use claude mcp add to register new servers
practices.mistakesOk: Create custom skills to teach Claude your tool workflows

tip9.quote: Claude + CLI tools + MCP = a development workflow that covers everything.

tip10.title: Create Custom Skills and Subagents
tip10.description: Build reusable skills (SKILL.md files) for domain knowledge and custom subagents for specialized tasks.

practices.maxTasks: Create skills for team conventions and code standards
practices.cascadeMethod: Build subagents for code review, debugging, data analysis
practices.consistentDirection: Share skills and agents across your team via plugins
practices.checkOldest: Use /agents command to create subagents interactively

tip10.quote: Skills and subagents turn Claude from a generic assistant into a domain expert.

tip11.title: Avoid Common Failure Patterns
tip11.description: Know the anti-patterns that lead to poor results and avoid them proactively.
tip11.command: Too much context, vague instructions, no verification, ignoring Plan Mode for complex tasks

practices.shortAlias: Don't dump entire codebases into context
practices.continueFlag: Don't give vague instructions like 'make it better'
practices.resumeFlag: Don't skip verification — always include test/lint/check commands

tip11.quote: Most failures come from too much context, not too little.

clearInstructions.title: Alias 'claude' to 'c'
clearInstructions.text: Add 'alias c=claude' to your shell config. Use 'c -c' to continue, 'c -r' to resume.

incrementalDev.title: Use Voice Input
incrementalDev.text: Hold Space to dictate. Claude handles transcription mistakes gracefully.

testFirst.title: Multiple Sessions
testFirst.text: Run multiple Claude instances for parallel tasks. Use git worktrees for isolation.

codeReview.title: Pass Context Between Sessions
codeReview.text: Ask Claude to write a HANDOFF.md before starting fresh. Load it in the next session.

tips.conclusion: Claude Code is a genuinely transformative tool
tips.conclusionText: These tips come from real developers and official best practices. Start with verification criteria (tip #1) and context management (tip #5). The productivity gains compound as you master each technique.

mcp.title: MCP Protocol Guide
mcp.subtitle: Connect Claude Code to external tools and services with Model Context Protocol.
mcp.heroSubtitle: Connect Claude Code to external tools and services with Model Context Protocol.

whatIsMcp.title: What is MCP?
whatIsMcp.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that connects Claude Code to external tools and data sources. Think of it as a USB port for AI — plug in databases, APIs, browsers, and any custom tool to extend Claude's capabilities.

features.monitoring: Any tool with an MCP server

example.title: How It Works
example.description: Use 'claude mcp add' to connect servers, or configure .mcp.json in your project. Claude discovers available tools and uses them when relevant to your request.

httpTransport.title: Transport Protocols
httpTransport.description: HTTP is the recommended transport. SSE (Server-Sent Events) is deprecated. Also supports stdio for local processes and WebSocket (ws) for real-time connections.

resourceMentions.title: MCP Resource Mentions
resourceMentions.description: Use @ syntax to reference MCP resources directly in your prompts. Claude resolves the resource and includes its content.

slashCommands.title: MCP Prompts as Slash Commands
slashCommands.description: MCP server prompts are automatically registered as slash commands. Type / to see available commands from connected servers.

mcpSearch.title: MCP Tool Search (Auto Mode)
mcpSearch.description: When MCP tools exceed 10% of context, they are automatically deferred. Claude searches for the right tool when needed, saving significant context window space.
mcpSearch.config: Configure threshold: mcpToolSearch: 'auto:N' where N = 0-100 percent

configuration.title: Configuration Methods
configuration.subagentConfig: Subagent: mcpServers in agent frontmatter (scoped to agent)

popularServers.title: Popular MCP Servers
popularServers.subtitle: The most useful MCP servers for development workflows:

supabase.title: Supabase MCP
supabase.description: Direct database operations, schema management, Edge Functions, and real-time subscriptions.

github.title: GitHub MCP
github.description: Complete GitHub integration for repositories, issues, pull requests, and actions.

slack.title: Slack MCP
slack.description: Team communication, channel management, and automated notifications.

postgresql.title: PostgreSQL MCP
postgresql.description: Advanced database operations, performance tuning, and data analysis.

registry.title: MCP Server Registry
registry.description: Browse hundreds of community MCP servers at the official registry. Find servers for any tool or service.

proTip.title: Pro Tip
proTip.description: Start with GitHub and Supabase MCPs for immediate value. Use MCP Tool Search to keep context lean when connecting many servers.

practicalUsage.title: Ready to Get Started with MCP?
practicalUsage.description: Explore our tutorials to set up MCP servers and build powerful automated workflows.

supabaseTutorial.title: Master Supabase MCP
supabaseTutorial.description: Connect Supabase to Claude Code and automate your entire backend development workflow.

quickSetup.title: Quick Setup
quickSetup.command: claude mcp add supabase -e SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=your_token -- npx -y @supabase/mcp-server-supabase@latest
quickSetup.description: Connect with your Supabase personal access token (generate from Settings > Access Tokens)

example1.command: Create a table to store blog posts
example1.result: Schema design > Table creation > RLS policy setup

example2.command: Create and deploy user authentication Edge Function

example3.command: Analyze current database usage and costs
example3.result: Usage query > Cost calculation > Optimization suggestions

example4.command: Set up real-time subscriptions for users table
example4.result: Enable Realtime > Configure subscription > Create event listeners

proTip.title: Pro Tip
proTip.description: Advanced commands like 'Analyze database costs' and 'Optimize slow queries' are also available!

workflowAutomation.title: Complete Workflow Automation
workflowAutomation.description: Connect GitHub + Supabase + Slack to automate the full cycle: Issue > Code Fix > Deploy > Team Notification.

magicCommand.title: Full Automation Example
magicCommand.command: Analyze GitHub issue #42, fix it, deploy, and notify Slack #dev
workflow: Analyze issue content and find related code files
Write bug fix code and tests
Create PR on GitHub and merge
Send completion notification to Slack channel

example1.command: Backup database daily at 9 AM and notify Slack with results

example2.command: When new PR opens, automatically run tests and comment results

example3.command: If database performance slows, auto-optimize and report to team

realWorldExample.title: Real-World Case
realWorldExample.description: Teams report reducing bug fix time from hours to minutes using MCP-powered automation workflows.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Domain expertise defined via SKILL.md files that Claude applies automatically

whatAreSkills.title: What are Skills?
whatAreSkills.description: A Skill is a SKILL.md file with YAML frontmatter that teaches Claude how to do something specific. When you ask Claude something matching a Skill's description, it automatically applies the skill's instructions, tools, and context.
whatAreSkills.automaticDesc: Claude detects when a Skill is relevant and applies it automatically based on the description
whatAreSkills.markdownDesc: YAML frontmatter for metadata + markdown body for instructions and context
whatAreSkills.hotReloadDesc: Skills reload automatically when modified — no session restart needed

bundledSkills.title: Bundled Skills
bundledSkills.description: Claude Code comes with built-in skills:
bundledSkills.batch: /batch — Process multiple files or tasks in parallel using subagents
bundledSkills.claudeApi: /claude-api — Work with the Anthropic API using best practices
bundledSkills.simplify: /simplify — Simplify complex code while preserving behavior

locations.title: Where Skills Live
locations.priority: Priority order: Enterprise > Personal > Project > Plugin (higher wins on name conflicts)
locations.personal: Personal: ~/.claude/skills/ — applies to all your projects
locations.project: Project: .claude/skills/ — applies to the project (shared with team)

fields.name: name — Display name of the skill
fields.description: description — What the skill does (used for automatic matching)
fields.disableModelInvocation: disable-model-invocation — Prevent Claude from auto-invoking (manual /skill-name only)
fields.userInvocable: user-invocable — Whether users can invoke with /skill-name
fields.allowedTools: allowed-tools — List of tools the skill can use
fields.model: model — Override the model for this skill
fields.context: context — 'fork' to run in a subagent with separate context
fields.agent: agent — Reference a subagent to execute the skill
fields.hooks: hooks — Hook definitions scoped to this skill's lifecycle

substitutions.title: String Substitutions
substitutions.description: Use dynamic values in your skill instructions:
substitutions.arguments: $ARGUMENTS — Full argument string passed to the skill

dynamicContext.title: Dynamic Context Injection
dynamicContext.description: Use !`command` syntax to inject command output into skill instructions at load time. Example: !`cat package.json` inserts your package.json contents.

permissions.title: Permission Control
permissions.description: Control who can invoke skills: Skill(name) for specific skill, Skill(name *) for skill with any arguments.

hooks.title: Skills with Hooks
hooks.description: Skills can define hooks that run during the skill's lifecycle. Hooks are scoped to the skill and automatically cleaned up when it finishes.

multiFile.title: Multi-File Skills (Progressive Disclosure)
multiFile.description: For complex skills, put essential info in SKILL.md and detailed docs in separate files. Claude reads the supporting files only when needed, keeping context lean.
multiFile.tip: Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines. Scripts in the skill directory can be executed without loading into context.

testing.title: Testing Your Skills
testing.testSkillDesc: Ask a question matching the skill description, or invoke directly with /skill-name:

cta.title: Start Building Skills
cta.description: Create your first skill to encode your team's standards, workflows, and domain knowledge.

subagents.title: Subagents
subagents.subtitle: Specialized AI assistants with independent context for task-specific workflows

whatAreSubagents.title: What are Subagents?
whatAreSubagents.description: Subagents are independent Claude instances with their own context windows, tool access, and specialized instructions. They can explore code, execute plans, run in the background, and return summarized results without consuming your main session's context.
whatAreSubagents.independentDesc: Each subagent has its own context window — they don't consume your main session's tokens
whatAreSubagents.specializedDesc: Configure model, tools, permissions, and instructions for specific workflows
whatAreSubagents.parallelDesc: Run subagents in the background (Ctrl+B) and check results when ready

explore.title: Explore
explore.description: Uses Haiku model, read-only tools. Fast, cheap exploration and code search.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Inherits current model, read-only tools. Analyzes code and creates plans without making changes.

general.title: General-Purpose
general.description: Has access to all tools. Used when you need a subagent that can read and write code.

bash.title: Bash
bash.description: Specialized for shell command execution and system operations.

creation.title: Creating Custom Subagents
creation.interactive: Use /agents command to create subagents interactively
creation.markdown: Or create markdown files directly in .claude/agents/ (project) or ~/.claude/agents/ (user)
creation.cliFlag: Load additional agents with --agents CLI flag (session scope)
creation.pluginAgents: Bundle agents in plugins for team distribution

frontmatter.title: Agent Frontmatter Fields
frontmatter.name: name — Display name of the subagent
frontmatter.description: description — What the agent does (used for automatic selection)
frontmatter.model: model — AI model: sonnet, opus, haiku, inherit, or full model ID
frontmatter.tools: tools — Allowed tools list. Use Agent(worker, researcher) syntax for nested agents
frontmatter.permissionMode: permissionMode — default, acceptEdits, dontAsk, bypassPermissions, plan
frontmatter.maxTurns: maxTurns — Maximum conversation turns before stopping
frontmatter.skills: skills — Preload specific skills into the subagent
frontmatter.mcpServers: mcpServers — MCP servers scoped to this agent (inline config or reference)
frontmatter.memory: memory — Persistent memory: user, project, or local scope
frontmatter.background: background — Run in background by default

mcpScoping.title: MCP Servers Scoped to Subagents
mcpScoping.description: Define MCP servers in agent frontmatter. The servers are only available to that specific subagent, keeping tool access controlled and context lean.

execution.title: Running Subagents
execution.flag: --agent flag to use a subagent for the entire session
execution.background: Ctrl+B to run a subagent in the background
execution.resume: Use SendMessage to resume or continue a subagent
execution.autoCompaction: Subagents support auto-compaction for long-running tasks

codeReviewer.title: Code Reviewer
codeReviewer.description: Reviews code changes, checks for bugs, style issues, and security vulnerabilities. Uses read-only tools and plan permission mode.

debugger.title: Debugger
debugger.description: Systematic debugging with access to bash, file reading, and test execution. Follows a structured debug workflow.

dataScientist.title: Data Scientist
dataScientist.description: Analyzes data, generates visualizations, and runs statistical tests. Scoped MCP server for database access.

dbReader.title: DB Reader with Hooks
dbReader.description: Read-only database agent with hooks that log all queries for audit purposes.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Package and distribute skills, agents, hooks, and MCP servers as reusable plugin bundles

whatArePlugins.title: What are Plugins?
whatArePlugins.description: Plugins are self-contained packages that bundle skills, agents, hooks, MCP configurations, and settings into a distributable unit. Share across your team or publish to the community marketplace.
whatArePlugins.customCommandsDesc: Skills are namespaced as /plugin-name:skill-name to avoid conflicts
whatArePlugins.teamSharingDesc: Share plugins via Git repos, registries, or the official marketplace
whatArePlugins.eventHooksDesc: Bundle hooks that respond to Claude Code lifecycle events
whatArePlugins.mcpIntegrationDesc: Include MCP server configurations and LSP server support

pluginStructure.title: Plugin Structure
pluginStructure.step1Desc: Define name, version, description, author, homepage, repository, and license
pluginStructure.step2Desc: SKILL.md files that are namespaced under the plugin name
pluginStructure.step4Desc: Hook scripts that respond to Claude Code events
pluginStructure.step5Desc: MCP servers bundled with the plugin
pluginStructure.step6Desc: Language Server Protocol servers for advanced language features
pluginStructure.step7Desc: Default configuration including the default agent for the plugin

customCommand.title: Plugin Manifest
customCommand.desc: Create .claude-plugin/plugin.json with name, description, version, author, homepage, repository, license

manifest.title: Testing Locally
manifest.desc: Use --plugin-dir to load your plugin during development. Use /reload-plugins for hot reload.

project.title: Project-Level
project.desc: Check into version control with your project

team.title: Team Registry
team.desc: Share across your organization via Git or internal registry

marketplace.title: Official Marketplace
marketplace.desc: Submit to the official marketplace at claude.ai/settings/plugins/submit

cli.title: Plugin Marketplace CLI
cli.search: Search for plugins in the marketplace
cli.install: Install a plugin from the marketplace

lspSupport.title: LSP Server Support
lspSupport.description: Plugins can bundle Language Server Protocol (LSP) servers via .lsp.json. Provides advanced language features, diagnostics, and IDE integration capabilities.

converting.title: Converting Standalone Configs to Plugins
converting.description: Already have skills, agents, or hooks in your project? Package them into a plugin for easy distribution. Move files into the plugin structure and create a manifest.

documentation.title: Document Thoroughly
documentation.desc: Provide clear README files explaining what your plugin does and how to use it

versioning.title: Use Semantic Versioning
versioning.desc: Follow semantic versioning (major.minor.patch) for compatibility management

testing.title: Test with --plugin-dir
testing.desc: Use --plugin-dir flag to load and test plugins locally before distribution

security.title: Security First
security.desc: Never include secrets in plugins. Use environment variables for sensitive data

cta.title: Ready to Create Your First Plugin?
cta.description: Package your skills, agents, hooks, and MCP configs into a distributable plugin.

hooks.title: Hooks
hooks.subtitle: Event-driven automation for Claude Code's entire lifecycle

whatAreHooks.title: What are Hooks?
whatAreHooks.description: Hooks are scripts or prompts that execute in response to specific events in Claude Code. They support four types: command (shell scripts), HTTP (webhook calls), prompt (LLM-powered decisions), and agent (subagent verification). Use them to enforce policies, automate workflows, and integrate external tools.
whatAreHooks.customizableDesc: Command, HTTP, Prompt, and Agent hooks for different needs
whatAreHooks.policiesEnforcementDesc: Enforce team standards, security policies, and code quality rules
whatAreHooks.integrationsDesc: Call webhooks, run scripts, and connect to external services

command.title: Command Hooks
command.description: Execute shell commands. Exit code 0 = success, 2 = blocking error, other = non-blocking warning.

http.title: HTTP Hooks
http.description: Send HTTP requests to webhooks or APIs. Configure headers and allowedEnvVars for authentication.

prompt.title: Prompt Hooks (LLM-Powered)
prompt.description: Describe what you want in natural language. An LLM evaluates the condition and returns a decision. Great for complex logic that's hard to code.

agent.title: Agent Hooks
agent.description: Spawn a subagent to verify or analyze. The subagent runs independently and returns its assessment.

sessionStart.desc: Fired when a Claude session starts. Initialize resources, check environment, load context.
sessionStart.useCase: Use case: Environment setup, dependency checks

sessionEnd.desc: Fired when a session ends. Clean up resources, save state, generate reports.
sessionEnd.useCase: Use case: Cleanup, analytics, session summaries

instructionsLoaded.desc: Fired when CLAUDE.md and instructions are loaded. Modify or augment instructions.

userPromptSubmit.desc: Fired when user submits a prompt. Modify, augment, or intercept prompts.
userPromptSubmit.useCase: Use case: Context injection, prompt enhancement

preToolUse.desc: Fired before Claude uses any tool. Validate, modify, or block tool usage.
preToolUse.useCase: Use case: Security validation, file protection

permissionRequest.desc: Fired when a permission is requested. Auto-approve, deny, or log requests.
permissionRequest.useCase: Use case: Auto-approval policies, audit logging

postToolUse.desc: Fired after a tool completes successfully. Process results, trigger notifications.

postToolUseFailure.desc: Fired after a tool fails. Handle errors, retry, or alert.
postToolUseFailure.useCase: Use case: Error recovery, failure alerts

subagentStart.desc: Fired when a subagent is about to start. Monitor, inject context, or block.
subagentStart.useCase: Use case: Subagent monitoring, context injection

subagentStop.desc: Fired when a subagent completes. Process results, chain tasks, notify.
subagentStop.useCase: Use case: Result processing, task chaining

notification.desc: Fired when Claude sends a notification. Customize notification behavior.
notification.useCase: Use case: Custom alerts, team notifications

stop.desc: Fired when Claude stops generating. Final checks and validations.
stop.useCase: Use case: Final verification, quality checks

stopFailure.desc: Fired when Claude fails to stop cleanly. Error recovery.

preCompact.desc: Fired before context compaction. Save important information before context is compressed.
preCompact.useCase: Use case: Context preservation, important data backup

postCompact.desc: Fired after context compaction. Restore or inject critical context.

teammateIdle.desc: Fired when a teammate in Agent Teams becomes idle.
teammateIdle.useCase: Use case: Task assignment, load balancing

taskCompleted.desc: Fired when a task is completed in Agent Teams.

configChange.desc: Fired when configuration changes. React to setting updates.

worktreeCreate.desc: Fired when a git worktree is created.
worktreeCreate.useCase: Use case: Worktree setup, environment initialization

worktreeRemove.desc: Fired when a git worktree is removed.

elicitation.desc: Fired when Claude asks the user a question.

elicitationResult.desc: Fired when the user responds to an elicitation.

setup.desc: Triggered via --init, --init-only, or --maintenance flags. For repository setup and maintenance.
setup.useCase: Use case: Environment setup, dependency checks, project initialization

configuration.title: Configuration Locations
configuration.plugin: Plugin hooks/ directory — Bundled in plugins
configuration.skillAgent: Skill/agent frontmatter — Scoped to skill or agent lifecycle

exitCodes.title: Exit Codes (Command Hooks)
exitCodes.blocking: Exit 2 — Blocking error, stop the action
exitCodes.warning: Other codes — Non-blocking warning, continue with message

envVars.title: Environment Variables
envVars.envFile: CLAUDE_ENV_FILE — Path to write environment variables for Claude to read

matchers.title: Matcher Patterns
matchers.description: Each event supports matcher patterns to filter when hooks fire. For example, PreToolUse matchers filter by tool name, PostToolUse matchers filter by tool name and result.

promptBasedHooks.title: Prompt-Based Hooks
promptBasedHooks.description: Use LLM-powered decision making in hooks. Describe what you want in natural language instead of writing code. The LLM evaluates the condition and returns a structured decision.
promptBasedHooks.useCase1: Complex decision logic that's hard to write in code

autoFormat.title: Auto-Format Code After Edits
autoFormat.desc: Automatically run Prettier on files after Claude edits them

fileProtection.title: Protect Sensitive Files
fileProtection.desc: Prevent Claude from editing critical configuration files

slackNotify.title: Send Slack Notifications via HTTP Hook
slackNotify.desc: Call a webhook when deployment happens

agentVerification.title: Agent Verification Hook
agentVerification.desc: Spawn a subagent to review code changes before they are committed

security.title: Security Considerations
security.description: Hooks execute code on your system. Follow these security best practices:
security.item1: Never store secrets in hook files — use environment variables
security.item2: Validate all inputs before executing external commands
security.item3: Review hooks from external sources before using them
security.item4: Use try-catch blocks to handle errors gracefully
security.item5: Use exit code 2 for security-critical blocking hooks

keepSimple.title: Keep Hooks Simple
keepSimple.desc: Each hook should do one thing well. Complex logic can slow down Claude.

errorHandling.title: Handle Errors Gracefully
errorHandling.desc: Always use try-catch blocks and provide meaningful error messages.

async.title: Use Async Hooks
async.desc: Hooks support async execution for external API calls and long-running operations.

testing.title: Test Your Hooks
testing.desc: Use /hooks command to browse and test hooks interactively.

cta.title: Ready to Automate Your Workflow?
cta.description: Start creating hooks to enforce standards, automate tasks, and integrate external tools.

agentTeams.title: Agent Teams
agentTeams.subtitle: Coordinate multiple Claude Code agents working together on complex tasks
agentTeams.experimentalNote: Agent Teams requires the CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS environment variable to be enabled.

whatAreTeams.title: What are Agent Teams?
whatAreTeams.description: Agent Teams is an experimental feature that allows multiple Claude Code agents to collaborate on complex tasks. A lead agent orchestrates teammates, distributing work through a shared task list and mailbox system.
whatAreTeams.experimentalDesc: Agent Teams is currently experimental and may change significantly
whatAreTeams.teamLeadDesc: One agent coordinates the team, assigns tasks, and synthesizes results
whatAreTeams.teammatesDesc: Independent agents that work in parallel on assigned tasks
whatAreTeams.taskListDesc: All agents see and update a shared task list for coordination
whatAreTeams.mailboxDesc: Agents communicate via a shared mailbox for questions and updates

architecture.title: Team Architecture
architecture.leadDesc: The orchestrator that breaks down tasks, assigns work to teammates, and synthesizes results
architecture.teammatesDesc: Independent agent instances that work on assigned subtasks in parallel
architecture.taskListDesc: A shared list of tasks that the lead creates and teammates pick up
architecture.mailboxDesc: Communication channel between the lead and teammates for status updates and results

setup.title: Setting Up Teams
setup.description: Configure agent teams using the /teams command or programmatically through the SDK.

displayModes.title: Display Modes
displayModes.inProcessDesc: All agents run within a single terminal session. Best for simpler coordination tasks with fewer agents.
displayModes.tmuxDesc: Each agent runs in a separate tmux pane, providing visual oversight of all agents working simultaneously. Recommended for complex multi-agent workflows.
displayModes.iterm2: iTerm2 Split Panes: Native iTerm2 split panes for macOS users

useCasesSection.title: Use Cases
useCasesSection.largeRefactoringDesc: Coordinate agents to refactor different modules simultaneously while maintaining consistency
useCasesSection.parallelTestingDesc: Run test suites across multiple environments or configurations in parallel
useCasesSection.codeReviewDesc: Assign different reviewers for security, performance, style, and correctness
useCasesSection.multiServiceDesc: Develop frontend and backend services simultaneously with coordinated APIs

bestPracticesSection.title: Best Practices
bestPracticesSection.tip1: Start with small teams (2-3 agents) and scale up as needed
bestPracticesSection.tip2: Give each teammate a clear, focused task with well-defined boundaries
bestPracticesSection.tip3: Use tmux mode for better visibility when running more than 2 agents
bestPracticesSection.tip4: Monitor the mailbox regularly for coordination issues
bestPracticesSection.tip5: Set appropriate timeouts for long-running tasks
bestPracticesSection.tip6: Use the lead agent to verify and integrate teammate outputs

creating.title: Creating a Team
creating.description: Define a team lead agent with teammate definitions. Each teammate has its own model, tools, and instructions.
creating.tasksPerAgent: Aim for 5-6 tasks per teammate for optimal throughput

controlling.title: Controlling Teammates
controlling.description: The team lead assigns tasks, monitors progress, and can redirect teammates. Teammates report back via the mailbox when tasks are complete or when they need help.
controlling.permissions: Teammates inherit permission settings from the team lead by default

parallelReview.title: Parallel Code Review
parallelReview.description: Multiple agents review different parts of a codebase simultaneously — one checks security, another checks performance, another checks style.

competingHypotheses.title: Competing Hypotheses Debugging
competingHypotheses.description: Spawn multiple agents to investigate different theories about a bug in parallel. The first to find the root cause wins.

largeRefactor.title: Large-Scale Refactoring
largeRefactor.description: Split a large refactoring task across agents, each handling a different module or file group.

bestPractices.title: Best Practices
bestPractices.teamSize: Keep teams small (3-5 agents) — larger teams have more coordination overhead
bestPractices.taskGranularity: Make tasks independent — agents work best when they don't need to coordinate frequently
bestPractices.clearInstructions: Give each teammate clear, self-contained instructions
bestPractices.monitorProgress: Use the task list to monitor progress and adjust assignments
bestPractices.worktrees: Use git worktrees for isolation when agents modify the same files

items.experimental: Feature is experimental and may change
items.contextIndependent: Each agent has independent context — no shared memory
items.coordination: Complex inter-agent coordination can be unreliable
items.cost: Running multiple agents increases API costs proportionally

desktopApp.title: Desktop App
desktopApp.subtitle: Claude Code in a native desktop experience with visual diffs, multiple sessions, and more

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The Claude Code Desktop App provides a native GUI environment with visual diffs, multiple concurrent sessions, scheduled tasks, and a more accessible interface for developers who prefer graphical tools.

download.title: Download
download.description: Available for macOS, Windows, and Linux
download.systemReqsList: macOS 12+, Windows 10+, or Ubuntu 20.04+ with 4GB RAM minimum

features.title: Key Features
features.visualDiffsDesc: See code changes with rich side-by-side visual diffs before accepting
features.multipleSessionsDesc: Run multiple Claude Code sessions simultaneously in separate tabs
features.schedulingDesc: Schedule recurring tasks like code reviews, dependency updates, and report generation
features.fileExplorerDesc: Browse your project files with an integrated file explorer
features.historyDesc: Access complete history of past sessions with search and filtering
features.themesDesc: Choose from light, dark, and custom themes for your preferred look

gettingStartedSection.title: Getting Started
gettingStartedSection.step1: Download and install the Desktop App for your platform
gettingStartedSection.step2: Sign in with your Anthropic account
gettingStartedSection.step3: Open a project folder or create a new workspace
gettingStartedSection.step4: Start coding with Claude in the visual interface

cliVsDesktop.title: CLI vs Desktop App
cliVsDesktop.description: Both the CLI and Desktop App run the same Claude Code engine. Choose based on your workflow preferences.

rows.desktop: Native GUI with panels and tabs

cliVsDesktop.recommendation: Use the CLI for scripting, CI/CD pipelines, and headless automation. Use the Desktop App for day-to-day development where visual feedback and session management matter.

uniqueCapabilities.title: Desktop-Exclusive Capabilities
uniqueCapabilities.description: Features available only in the Desktop App that enhance the visual development experience.

items.title: Visual Diff Review
items.description: Review every code change in a rich side-by-side diff viewer. Accept, reject, or edit individual hunks before they are applied to your files.

items.title: Tabbed Session Management
items.description: Open multiple Claude Code sessions in separate tabs. Work on a feature in one tab while debugging in another, each with independent context.

items.title: Built-in Task Scheduler
items.description: Schedule tasks like daily dependency updates, weekly code reviews, or nightly test runs directly from the UI without external cron jobs.

items.title: Drag-and-Drop File Context
items.description: Drag files or folders from your OS file manager directly into the chat to add them as context. No need to type file paths.

items.title: Integrated File Explorer
items.description: Browse your project tree in a sidebar panel. Click any file to view it or add it to context without leaving the app.

items.title: Session History Browser
items.description: Search, filter, and revisit past sessions with full conversation history. Resume any previous session with one click.

installation.title: Installation Methods
installation.description: Multiple ways to install the Claude Code Desktop App on your system.
installation.macosDirectDownloadDesc: Download the .dmg installer from the official site
installation.windowsDirectDownloadDesc: Download the .exe installer from the official site
installation.linuxDirectDownloadDesc: Download the .deb or .AppImage from the official site
installation.linuxShellInstallDesc: Install via the official install script

keyboardShortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
keyboardShortcuts.description: Speed up your workflow with these essential keyboard shortcuts.

webApp.title: Web App
webApp.subtitle: Use Claude Code directly in your browser without any installation

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Claude Code on the web brings all the capabilities of Claude Code to your browser. No installation needed — just open your browser and start AI-assisted coding. Perfect for quick tasks, remote machines, or environments where local installation isn't possible.

features.title: Key Features
features.noInstallDesc: Access Claude Code from any browser without installing anything locally
features.cloudWorkspacesDesc: Create and manage workspaces in the cloud with persistent storage
features.collaborationDesc: Share sessions with team members for AI-assisted pair programming
features.gitIntegrationDesc: Connect directly to GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket repositories
features.terminalAccessDesc: Full terminal access for running commands and scripts in the browser
features.mobileFriendlyDesc: Responsive design that works on tablets and mobile devices

gettingStartedSection.title: Getting Started
gettingStartedSection.step2: Sign in with your Anthropic account
gettingStartedSection.step3: Create a new workspace or connect an existing repository
gettingStartedSection.step4: Start coding with Claude in the browser

limitations.title: Considerations
limitations.note2: Some filesystem operations may be slower than local installation
limitations.note3: Large project support depends on internet speed

bestPractices.title: Best Practices
bestPractices.subtitle: Proven strategies and techniques for getting the most out of Claude Code

contextManagement.title: Context Management
contextManagement.description: Effective context management is the key to getting accurate and relevant responses from Claude Code.
contextManagement.tip1Desc: Only include relevant files and information in your context window. Use CLAUDE.md to provide project-specific guidance. Start fresh sessions for new topics with /clear.
contextManagement.tip2Desc: Use /compact to summarize the conversation while preserving key information when context gets large. Use /btw for side questions that don't pollute main context.
contextManagement.tip3Desc: Use CLAUDE.md at project and user level to persist important context across sessions. Write HANDOFF.md before starting fresh to migrate context between sessions.

verification.title: Verification & Testing
verification.description: Always verify Claude Code output to ensure correctness and quality.
verification.tip1Desc: Always review code changes before accepting them. Use visual diffs to understand what changed. Create draft PRs to review everything before merging.
verification.tip2Desc: Ask Claude to run tests after making changes. Set up autotest hooks for automatic verification. Include test commands in your prompts.
verification.tip3Desc: Leverage git checkpoints to safely experiment and roll back with /rewind. Ask Claude to double-check its claims and use /chrome for visual verification of web apps.

planMode.title: Plan Mode & Structured Thinking
planMode.description: Use Plan Mode for complex tasks that benefit from structured analysis before implementation.
planMode.tip1Desc: For complex features, press Shift+Tab to enter Plan Mode. Let Claude analyze the problem before writing code. Review the plan before approving execution.
planMode.tip2Desc: Divide complex work into smaller, manageable steps. This improves accuracy and makes it easier to verify each piece. Use subagents for isolated investigation.
planMode.tip3Desc: Enable extended thinking for complex architectural decisions that require deep reasoning. Use 'think', 'think hard', or 'ultrathink' keywords.

promptEngineering.title: Prompt Engineering
promptEngineering.description: How you communicate with Claude Code significantly affects the quality of results.
promptEngineering.tip1Desc: Provide clear, specific instructions including file paths, function names, and expected behavior. Say 'fix the null reference in handleSubmit in user.tsx' not 'fix the error'.
promptEngineering.tip2Desc: Show Claude examples of the pattern or style you want. Use @ to reference specific files. Paste screenshots of errors, UIs, or designs. Include success criteria.
promptEngineering.tip3Desc: Tell Claude about project constraints, coding standards, and patterns to follow. Include verification steps: 'all tests should pass and the linter should be clean'.

scaling.title: Scaling with Claude Code
scaling.description: Techniques for using Claude Code effectively on larger projects and teams.
scaling.tip1Desc: Integrate Claude Code into your CI/CD pipeline with claude -p for automated code reviews, test generation, and documentation updates.
scaling.tip2Desc: Use shared CLAUDE.md files for consistent behavior across team members. Create custom skills and subagents. Share via plugins for team distribution.
scaling.tip3Desc: Use subagents to fan out across files. Use Agent Teams (experimental) for coordinated parallel work. Use git worktrees for isolation between agents.

thirdPartyTools.title: Third-Party Tools
thirdPartyTools.subtitle: Explore open-source alternatives and extensions in the Claude Code ecosystem

ecosystem.title: The Claude Code Ecosystem
ecosystem.description: Beyond the official Claude Code CLI, a vibrant ecosystem of third-party tools has emerged. These tools offer different approaches, model flexibility, and specialized features.

opencode.description: An open-source Claude Code alternative that supports 75+ model providers including Claude, OpenAI, Groq, and local models via Ollama.

features.title: Key Features
features.localModels: Native local model support via Ollama
features.freeTool: Free tool — only pay for API usage

ohmyopencode.description: A powerful orchestration layer built on top of OpenCode, featuring specialized agents, multi-agent parallel execution, and 20+ built-in workflow automation hooks.

bestFor.title: Which Tool is Best For You?

claudeCode.desc: Best for teams prioritizing performance, security, and official support.

opencode.desc: Best for developers wanting provider flexibility and cost control.

ohmyopencode.desc: Best for complex projects requiring specialized agent orchestration.

cta.title: Ready to Explore?
cta.description: Start with the official Claude Code, or explore the open-source alternatives based on your needs.

useCases.title: Real-World Use Cases
useCases.subtitle: Discover how teams and individuals use Claude Code to maximize development productivity with real case studies.
useCases.heroSubtitle: Discover how teams and individuals use Claude Code to maximize development productivity with real case studies.

backend.title: Backend Development Team
backend.description: API development, database management, and system architecture with Claude Code. Use MCP servers for direct database access and subagents for parallel code review.

achievements.title: Key Achievements
achievements.sql: 50% faster SQL query optimization via MCP
achievements.api: Automated API documentation with custom skills
achievements.logs: Intelligent log analysis with extended thinking

backend.tip: Connect PostgreSQL or Supabase MCP for direct database operations. Use Plan Mode for complex schema migrations.

frontend.title: Frontend Development Team
frontend.description: UI component development, responsive design, and performance optimization. Paste screenshots to generate code from designs.

achievements.title: Key Achievements
achievements.design: 60% reduction in design system build time
achievements.components: Component library generation with test suites

frontend.tip: Paste screenshots or design mockups directly into Claude. Use /chrome for visual verification of generated UI.

devops.title: DevOps Team
devops.description: CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure automation, and monitoring. Use hooks for deployment automation and MCP for Slack notifications.

achievements.title: Key Achievements
achievements.security: Automated security vulnerability detection with subagents

devops.tip: Set up hooks for deployment notifications and use Agent Teams for parallel infrastructure review.

data.title: Data Engineering Team
data.description: ETL pipelines, data analysis, and ML model development. Create custom data-scientist subagents with scoped database MCP access.

achievements.title: Key Achievements
achievements.pipeline: 70% reduction in data pipeline development time
achievements.analysis: Complex SQL query optimization via MCP
achievements.ml: Accelerated ML model prototyping with custom skills

data.tip: Create a data-scientist subagent with read-only database MCP access and custom analysis skills.

qa.title: QA Engineer
qa.description: Test automation, bug reproduction, and quality assurance with Claude Code. Use the /loop skill for iterative test refinement.
qa.achievement: Test coverage increased to 85%, bug discovery time reduced by 60%

documentation.title: Technical Writer
documentation.description: API documentation, user guides, and tutorial creation. Create custom documentation skills for consistent output.
documentation.achievement: Documentation time reduced by 70%, consistent quality across all docs

startup.title: Startup Founder
startup.description: MVP development, prototyping, and rapid feature implementation. Use Agent Teams for parallel development across features.
startup.achievement: MVP development time reduced from 2 months to 3 weeks

ux.title: UX Designer
ux.description: Prototype implementation, usability testing, and design system creation. Paste mockups and get working code.
ux.achievement: Design-to-code time reduced by 50% with screenshot-based workflows

individual.title: Individual Developer
individual.description: Side projects, learning, and portfolio building. Use skills and subagents to build your personal development toolkit.
individual.achievement: Weekly development time reduced from 20 hours to 8 hours

education.title: Educational Institution
education.description: Teaching programming, collaborative learning, and code review. Use Plan Mode for teaching problem decomposition.
education.achievement: Student engagement increased by 80%, learning outcomes improved significantly

productivity.title: Core Productivity Strategies
productivity.goals: Clear Goals: Provide specific objectives and success criteria before starting
productivity.progress: Explore First: Use Plan Mode to understand and plan before coding
productivity.feedback: Verify Always: Include tests, linting, and type checking in every request
productivity.documentation: Manage Context: Use /clear, /compact, and subagents to keep context fresh

teamStrategies.title: Team-Specific Strategies
teamStrategies.development: Development Team: Custom subagents for code review, shared skills for standards
teamStrategies.design: Design Team: Screenshot-to-code workflows, /chrome for visual verification
teamStrategies.data: Data Team: MCP servers for databases, custom data analysis skills
teamStrategies.qa: QA Team: /loop skill for iterative testing, hooks for CI/CD integration
teamStrategies.individual: Individual Dev: Custom aliases, personal skills in ~/.claude/skills/

cta.title: Boost Your Productivity with Claude Code Today
cta.description: Build features faster, fix bugs quicker, and automate everything from PRs to deployments.
